sources_audited.note: The O*NET record supports the stated mixture of batch recording, process monitoring, recipe execution, equipment operation, cleaning, material handling, and product-quality tasks.

BLS lists equipment setup and control, mix and gauge monitoring, batch-data recording, cleaning, and final-product quality checks, and states that automatic weighing and mixing can require fewer machine operators.

The paper states that commercial malt houses perform analysis for process and product quality control and trade specifications, that automation is employed in most malt-testing facilities, and that multiple routine assays are automated.

The cited Census profile resolves, but its dynamically rendered content did not expose the claimed 42 employer establishments for 2023 in the reopened page. In any event, an establishment profile does not establish firm ownership, eligibility, or operator-retention rates.
Gallup reports a fall-2024 succession study with 1,264 working-owner respondents, a 22% five-year sell-or-transfer plan rate among employer-business owners, and the cited 52.3% age statistic.
The Brewers Association reports 2024 craft volume down 3.9%, the overall U.S. beer market down 1.2% by volume, and rising ingredient costs and saturated-market competition for small brewers.
USDA ERS reports beer production down more than 13% from 2016/17 to 2023/24 and forecasts 2024/25 barley food, alcohol, and industrial use at 110.7 million bushels, 27% below 2016/17.

caveats: The labor-opportunity estimate is anchored to broad food-batchmaker and food-processing occupations rather than a wage-weighted 311213 workforce, so the mapping from cited tasks to a = 0.28 remains judgmental despite an explicit and directionally honest proxy bridge.

S3 is vendor-hosted and concerns laboratory analysis rather than whole-plant operations; it supports existing malt-testing automation but cannot validate plant-wide AI implementation or labor effects.

The dynamically rendered S4 page did not expose the cited 42-establishment figure when reopened, and establishment counts cannot determine independent-firm ownership, LMM eligibility, or transferable control status.
The S5 succession rate is a disclosed all-employer-business proxy based on intentions that may include nonmarket transfers, not a realized five-year control-transfer rate for malt manufacturers.

S6 and S7 establish beer-market and barley-use headwinds, but neither measures merchant-malt shipment demand or post-productivity price pass-through; q and d5 therefore remain broad, honestly labeled judgments.
summary: Mechanics pass and the record is publishable with caveats. The cited sources substantiate the reported occupational, laboratory-automation, succession, beer-volume, and barley-use facts, while the Census count was not visible in the reopened dynamic page. The primitive states and bridges are generally honest, the lens is coherent, and the narrative follows the score. Evidence remains estimate- and proxy-heavy, but no identified defect is material under the required test: with the frozen dataset input n = 0, F and L remain zero across all researched-input scenarios, so correcting or downgrading the cited research inputs would not change the STRUCTURAL_OUT base tier or the MODEL_CONDITIONED action category.
